title: nyssa sylvatica subsp. biflora
intro: Nyssa sylvatica subsp. biflora, also known as the two-flowered tupelo, is a subspecies of tree native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its unique two-flowered inflorescences. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Nyssa sylvatica subsp. biflora include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include making teas, tinctures, and infusions from its leaves, bark, and roots, which can be used to treat various health issues.
